en.extract:
The white-breasted whistler (Pachycephala lanioides) is a species of bird in the family Pachycephalidae.
It is endemic to Australia. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.


Taxonomy and systematics
Alternate names for the white-breasted whistler include white-bellied thickhead and white-bellied whistler. The latter name  should not be confused with the species of the same name, Pachycephala leucogastra.


Subspecies
Three subspecies are recognized:

P. l. carnarvoni - (Mathews, 1913): Found in coastal western Australia
P. l. lanioides - Gould, 1840: Found in coastal northwestern Australia
P. l. fretorum - De Vis, 1889: Originally described as a separate species. Found in coastal northern Australia

The '''white-breasted whistler''' ('''''Pachycephala lanioides''''') is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Pachycephalidae]].
It is [[Endemism|endemic]] to [[Australia]]. Its natural [[habitat]] is subtropical or tropical mangrove [[forest]]s.

==Taxonomy and systematics==
Alternate names for the white-breasted whistler include '''white-bellied thickhead''' and '''white-bellied whistler'''. The latter name  should not be confused with the species of the same name, ''[[Pachycephala leucogastra]]''.

===Subspecies===
Three subspecies are recognized:<ref name=":0">{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.3|doi-access=free}}</ref>
* ''P. l. carnarvoni'' - <small>([[Gregory Mathews|Mathews]], 1913)</small>: Found in coastal [[western Australia]]	
* ''P. l. lanioides'' - <small>[[John Gould|Gould]], 1840</small>: Found in coastal [[northwestern Australia]]	
* ''P. l. fretorum'' - <small>[[Charles Walter De Vis|De Vis]], 1889</small>: Originally described as a separate species. Found in coastal [[northern Australia]]

zh.extract:
白胸啸鹟（学名：Pachycephala lanioides），是啸鹟科啸鹟属的一种，为澳大利亚的特有种。该物种的保护状况被评为无危。
白胸啸鹟的平均体重约为41.0克。栖息地包括亚热带或热带的湿润低地林和亚热带或热带的红树林。
